corpus_binding.interpretation: Explicit start/end tags, no inserted whitespace, depth-first traversal, and ascending arena-index sibling order are Rusty LSL local candidate policy. They assign no stream-info, info, desc, endpoint, official-liblsl, wire, round-trip, or compatibility role.

serialization.source_relationship: Borrows one accepted XmlElementTree without mutation or consumption and returns private accepted state containing exactly the selected byte limit and one owned String.
serialization.spelling: Every node emits an explicit start tag and end tag; no self-closing syntax or whitespace is inserted; accepted XmlCharacterData is emitted verbatim.
serialization.order: Containers emit direct children depth-first with siblings selected by ascending original arena index.
serialization.allocation: Checked exact output length and limit rejection precede one exact fallible traversal-frame-stack reserve and one exact fallible String reserve; one forward index pass enables linear traversal.

does_not_prove: parsing, decoding, logical-text recovery, canonicalization, source consumption, mutation, reverse conversion, or round trips
complete XML documents, declarations, byte-order marks, attributes, namespaces, mixed content, CDATA sections, comments, processing instructions, schemas, queries, or general DOM behavior
StreamDefinition or StreamDescriptor projection, core field spelling, info or desc roles, stream-info meaning, LSL field mapping, endpoint representation, official-liblsl behavior, or compatibility
dependency, feature, unsafe, I/O, protocol, wire, discovery, transport, runtime, adapter, provider, FFI, device, or authority behavior
